subject: BUSINESS MANAGEMENT
preamble: The course in Business Management at the senior school level embraces principles and activities in the core functional areas of business management. It also covers legal environment of business, information technology, globalization, economic integration and entrepreneurship.
objectives: Understanding and appreciation of the basic concepts, principles and practices of business management.
Understanding of the role of business management in an economy.
Ability to relate the concepts and principles of business management to practical business situations.
Ability to pursue further studies in business management.

examination_scheme.summary: There will be two papers, Paper 1 and Paper 2; both of which will constitute a composite paper and will be taken at a sitting.

papers.title: Multiple Choice Objectives
papers.details: This will consist of fifty compulsory multiple-choice objective questions which will cover the entire syllabus and will carry 30 marks. It will last 1 hour.

papers.title: Case Study and Essay Questions
papers.details: It will consist of a compulsory case study question and seven other essay questions, out of which candidates will be expected to answer any three. The case study will range between 200 and 250 words and will carry 25 marks, while the other questions will carry equal marks of 15 each. The paper will therefore carry a total of 70 marks.

topics.title: Forms of Business Organization
details: Identification of Major Forms: (Sole Proprietorship, Partnership, Limited Liability Company, Public Corporations, Co-operative society).
Procedures for the formations/Characteristics, advantages and disadvantages of each form.

topics.title: Business and Society
details: Ethical, legal and social responsibilities of Businesses.
Primary and Secondary stakeholders meaning and differences.

topics.title: Planning and Decision Making
details: Steps in planning, Types of planning, Limitations to planning.
Meaning and importance of decision making.
Steps in the decision making process.

topics.title: Organizing
details: Meaning, importance and activities of organizing.
Principles of organizing, Organizational structure and Organizational chart.
Centralization and decentralization- meaning, differences, advantages and disadvantages.
Reasons why managers do not delegate.
Reasons why subordinates do not accept delegation.

topics.title: Directing
details: Motivation - meaning, intrinsic and extrinsic factors.
Leadership styles and Sources of power available to leaders.
Communication - meaning, importance, formal and informal channels.

topics.title: Concept of ICT
details: Concept of Information and Communications Technology (ICT).

topics.title: Data and Information
details: Meaning, differences, and sources of business information.

topics.title: Functions of the Computer
details: Functions of the computer in business.

topics.title: Advantages and Challenges of Computers in Business
details: Advantages and challenges of the use of computers in business.

topics.title: Principles of Agency
details: Rights and duties of a principal.
Rights and duties of an agent.

topics.title: Negotiable Instruments
details: Meaning and characteristics of negotiable instruments.
Types of negotiable instruments (Cheques; Bills of exchange; Promissory notes).
Cheques: Class, types, advantages, disadvantages and ways of avoiding cheque frauds.

topics.title: Banking
details: Functions of commercial and other types of banks.

topics.title: Risk Management and Insurance
details: Meaning of risk and risk management.
Types of insurance and insurance policies.

syllabus_content.section_title: 6. ROLE OF GOVERNMENT IN THE ECONOMY

topics.title: Role of Government
details: Role of government in an economy.

topics.title: Government Revenue
details: Sources and types of government revenue.

topics.title: Taxation
details: Types of taxes and challenges of tax collection.

topics.title: Government Expenditure
details: Types and items of government expenditure.

syllabus_content.section_title: 7. INTERNATIONAL TRADE AND PROBLEMS OF DEVELOPING ECONOMIES

topics.title: International Trade
details: Difference between domestic and international trade.
Documents used in domestic and international trade.
Theories of absolute cost and comparative cost advantage.
Balance of Payments (BOP) and measures for correcting BOP deficit.

topics.title: Developing Economies
details: Challenges of developing economies in relation to international trade.
Ways of improving developing economies by government.

Role of institutions in international trade: International Monetary Fund (IMF), International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD), United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD).

topics.title: Economic Integration
details: Roles of regional bodies in economic integration: Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS), African Union (AU), European Union (EU), African Caribbean and Pacific Countries (ACP), Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).

topics.title: Elements of Human Resource Management
details: Steps in the recruitment and selection process.
Job Analysis (job description and job specification).

topics.title: Labour Relations
details: Meaning of labour relations, Trade Unions and industrial relations.
Collective bargaining - process, importance and rights of parties involved.

topics.title: Fundamentals of Production Management
details: Meaning of production and production management.
Forms of Production - extractive, manufacturing, construction, commercial and personal services.
Types of production process - job, batch, flow.
Steps in production planning and control.

topics.title: Principles of Marketing
details: Meaning of Marketing: Meaning and activities.
Elements of the marketing mix (product, price, promotion and place).
Stages of product life cycle (introduction, growth, maturity and decline).
Functions of packaging and uses of branding.
Factors affecting pricing and pricing objectives.

topics.title: Business Finance
details: Short-term and long-term sources of finance.
Government lending programmes; Venture capital fund.

topics.title: Entrepreneurship
details: Reasons for business successes and failures.
Record Keeping: Meaning, importance and types.
Time Management: Meaning, importance and techniques.

topics.title: Small Business
details: Procedures in the formation of small businesses.
Importance of small businesses in the economy.
Role of government in promoting small businesses.
Ways of sustaining an established business.

reading_list.author: Attieku, B., Marfo-Yiadom, E., Dorkey, T., and Tekyi, K.
reading_list.title: Business Management

reading_list.author: Browne, D., Barass, M.W., Dransfield, R., and Needham, D.
reading_list.title: Business Studies for AS Level

reading_list.author: Buckley, M., Brindley, B., and Greenwood, M.S.
reading_list.title: Business Studies

reading_list.author: Du Toit, G.S., Erasmus, B.G., and Strydom, J.W.
reading_list.title: Introduction to Business Management

reading_list.author: Nickels, W.G., McHugh, J.M., and McHugh, S.M.
reading_list.title: Understanding Business
